name: Deconstructed Chocolate Chip Cookie Sphere
description: A deconstructed chocolate chip cookie in a crisp chocolate sphere with a molten or ganache liquid center. Served with optional cocoa, chocolate drizzle, or ice cream.
keywords: chocolate, cookie, molten, dessert, sphere, advanced

winePairings.name: Tawny Port 20-year
winePairings.notes: Caramel + nut notes enhance molten center.

ingredients.note: Choose one for the center of the sphere. See sub-recipe

steps.instruction: Place chocolate hemispheres on a warm baking sheet to slightly melt edge.
Place a cookie dough ball or ganache inside one chocolate hemisphere.
Top with the matching hemisphere to form a complete sphere.
Chill briefly to set.
Plate: crack the sphere with a knife at the table for the deconstructed effect.
Optional plating: dust with cocoa powder, drizzle with chocolate sauce, or add a scoop of vanilla bean ice cream or caramel foam.  Or all of them!
Bring water to a simmer in a medium saucepan.
Place a glass or metal bowl on top of the saucepan.
Add about 75 g of the dark chocolate and melt until the temperature is 120°F (50°C).
Add in 25 g of chocolate, stirring contantly.
Pour tempered chocolate into silicone molds, coating evenly.
Scrape excess chocolate, refrigerate 5–10 minutes, then repeat steps 3 and 4 for a second layer for sturdiness.
Chill until firm, then carefully remove the chocolate hemispheres.
Cream butter and sugars until light and fluffy.
Mix in vanilla and egg yolk.
Sift in flour, baking soda, and salt; fold to combine.
Fold in chocolate chips.
Roll into small balls that fit inside the chocolate hemispheres.
Chill dough for at least 30 minutes to maintain shape during baking.

sub_recipes.name: Liquid Center - Eggless Ganache Core Option

ingredients.note: optional, like Baileys or coffee liqueur

steps.instruction: Heat cream until just simmering, pour over chocolate, stir until smooth.
Chill slightly to thicken but remain pourable.
Bake cookie dough balls for only 6–8 minutes at 355°F (180°C) so the centers remain gooey.
For extra decadence, roll the baked cookie dough balls in the Eggless Ganache.
Combine sugar and 1/2 cup of water in a small saucepan over medium-high heat.
Let simmer for 18-20 minutes or until it turns a light amber color.  Do not stir.
In a separate saucepan, boil the other 1/2 cup of water.
Once the amber color is achieved, carefully pour in the boiling water, whisking constantly.
Whisk in vanilla extract.
Let cool.
Combine the heavy cream, milk and sea salt with the cooled caramel syrup.
Using the foamer, spray directly onto the cookie.
